mic_hpf_fc: Fc of the input high-pass filter on the mic

mute_thresh_dB: RMS level in dB below which an input is considered muted
clip_thresh_dB: Level in dB above which an input is considered clipped

ave_coh_start_hz: Start frequency at which the average coherence is calculated
ave_coh_mid_hz: Frequency defining the boundry between vowel and consonants
ave_coh_end_hz: End frequency at which the average coherence is calculated
running_ave_window_ms: Time over which coherence is computed

rise_tc_ms: Rise time constant (rt60) for mic ref coupling factor
fall_tc_ms: Fall time constant (rt60) for mic ref coupling factor
min_coh: Coherence above which mic ref coupling factor adapts
max_coh_rise_tc_ms: Rise time constant (rt60) for mic ref coupling factor at max coherence
init_cf: Initial coupling factor between ref and mic

init_fast_adapt_time_period_ms: Period of time at beginning of call where noise estimate adapts quickly
init_begin_rise_tc_ms: Rise time constant (RT60) at beginning of call
init_end_rise_tc_ms: Rise time constant (RT60) at end of initial period of call
rise_dbps: Number of dB per second noise estimate is allowed to rise for duration of call
fall_tc_ms: Fall time constant (RT60) after initial period
offset_dB: A correction factor applied to published noise estimate to make it near-equivalent to average in power

min_ave_coh: Average coherence below which echo canceller is bypassed
min_coh: Bin by bin coherence below which adaptation freezes
adapt_exp: Adaptation factor raised to this power
blend_exp: E/Y blend ratio raised to this exponent
max_two_pass_adapt: Maximum adaptation factor used when combining pre and post adapt factors for 2nd pass

ml_model_frame_delay: DO NOT TUNE. Set manually.  Determines output frame delay of ml model in RES
prev_pwr_decay_rt60: Decay rate of residual output power after sudden suppression, as RT60
inst_coef_input_spec_rt60_hz: Instant coefficient input spectral smoothing coefficient RT60 specification in Hz
inst_coef_input_temp_rt60_ms: Instant coefficient input temporal smoothing coefficient RT60 specification in ms

min_frame_input_level_dBFS: Floor on the input frame rms measure
min_speech_input_level_dBFS: Do not boost when speech is below this level
speech_rms_level_rise_tc_ms: Number of ms for smooth speech rms to rise
speech_rms_level_fall_tc_ms: Number of ms for smooth speech rms to fall
post_speech_rms_level_rise_tc_ms: Number of ms for smooth speech rms to rise
post_speech_rms_level_fall_tc_ms: Number of ms for smooth speech rms to fall
speech_gain_rise_time_ms: Number of ms for speech gain to rise to target gain
speech_gain_fall_time_ms: Number of ms for speech gain to fall back to target gain
current_gain_rise_time_ms: Number of ms to rise to target (either speech or unity) gain
current_gain_fall_time_ms: Number of ms to fall back to target (either speech or unity) gain
current_gain_fall_in_speech_time_ms: Number of ms for gain to fall within speech
current_gain_freeze_post_speech_ms: Number of ms post speech before gain is allowed to fall
fixed_gain_dB: Start with this gain.  Nominal speech on unprocessed input is about -60dBFS
start_adaptive_speech_gain_dB: Start with this gain.  Nominal speech on unprocessed input is about -60dBFS
min_combined_speech_gain_dB: Don't apply a gain to speech that is less than this -- There should not be a reason to bring down the gain in human to machine
max_adaptive_speech_gain_dB: Don't apply a gain to speech that is more than this (-60 + 30 gets to minimum acceptable range)
vad_threshold: Threshold at which vad is considered speech

max_level_dBFS: Do not let signals go above this level
min_look_ahead_time_ms: Minimum amount of look-ahead given to gain logic.  Algorithmic delay
gain_release_tc_ms: Number of ms for gain to return to unity gain

init_adapt_time_period_ms: Period of time at beginning of call where noise estimate adapts quickly
init_begin_rise_tc_ms: Rise time constant (RT60) at beginning of call

bin_atten_dB: Maximum amount of bin-by-bin noise suppression, in dB
bin_min_recur_dB: Minimum amount of attenuation due to previous coefficient, in dB
bin_noise_overest_dB: Offset applied to noise estimate for Weiner calculation, in dB
fullband_atten_dB: Maximum amount of fullband noise suppression, in dB
fullband_noise_overest_dB: Offset applied to noise estimate for Weiner calculation, in dB
fullband_smooth_coef_rise_rt60: Rise time constant of smooth fullband coef
fullband_smooth_coef_fall_rt60: Fall time constant of smooth fullband coef

amplitude_dB: RMS level of output comfort noise in dB

init_phase_duration_ms: Duration of the initial phase in ms
xcc_high_delay_subsamp_threshold_ms: Threshold for when to use coarser subsampling. Higher is more CPU costly
corr_win_duration_ms: Duration of the correlation window in ms. Higher is more CPU costly
min_corr_coef: Minimum correlation before allowing it to be chosen as best delay
min_vad_update_ratio_in_each_band: Minimum ratio of VAD activity in each band, before allowing an update
xcc_denom_offset: Denominator offset in xcc calculation to prevent numerical issues, and to reduce impact from low energy signals
start_freq_hz_band_0: Start of first band in Hz
start_freq_hz_band_1: Start of second band in Hz
start_freq_hz_band_2: Start of third band in Hz
frac_delay_min_step_ms: Minimum required change before reporting a new value
frac_delay_smth_after_change_time_ms: Time in ms where we smooth less after a change
frac_delay_smth_after_change_time_const_: Time constant to apply to the smoothed frac delay after integer delay changes
frac_delay_smth_time_const_sec: Time constant to apply to the smoothed frac delay
vad_threshold_relative_dB: FE VAD threshold relative to the noise estimate
ref_noise_est_high_snr_dB: FE NL threshold for when to consider the signal to be high SNR
ref_noise_est_input_smth_time_constant_s: FE NL input smoothing time constant
ref_noise_est_init_smth_time_constant_se: FE NL initial smoothing time constant
ref_noise_est_high_snr_smth_time_constan: FE NL high SNR smoothing time constant
ref_noise_est_fast_smth_time_constant_se: FE NL fast adaptation time constant
ref_noise_est_init_time_ms: FE NL initial phase time in ms

vad_prob_onset_threshold: VAD probability onset threshold, below which, afe output is set to zero
vad_prob_offset_threshold: VAD probability offset threshold, below which, afe output is set to zero
smooth_vad_prob_rise_tc_sec: Rise time (sec) constant for VAD probability smoothing. Default is instant
smooth_vad_prob_fall_tc_sec: Fall time (sec) constant for VAD probability smoothing. Default is instant
init_output_noise_dB: Initial level of the smooth rms of the output noise estimate
rms_output_noise_adapt_rise_tc_sec: Rise time adaptation factor of output noise estimate
rms_output_noise_adapt_fall_tc_sec: Fall time adaptation factor of output noise estimate
vad_min_output_snr_dB: The output snr of the rms below which vad probability is defined as 0
vad_range_output_snr_dB: The range of output snr of the rms between min and max, where above max, vad probability is defined as 1
min_output_signal_dB: The lowest the measured signal level is allowed to drop to prevent the noise from falling in holes

playout_state_pause_to_duck_th_delta: Playout state threshold delta from pause to duck
playout_state_duck_to_inactive_th_delta: Playout state threshold delta from duck to inactive
playout_state_vad_smth_coef_time_constan: Tune/override for reaction time. Playout state VAD output smoothing
stt_segmentation_vad_smth_coef_time_cons: Tune/override for reaction time. STT segmentation VAD output smoothing
stt_segmentation_start_before_activity_s: STT segmentation time to start segment before detected activity
stt_segmentation_active_to_inactive_hang: STT segmentation active to inactive hangover in sec - MUST BE LESS THAN 1 second of zeros at end of file when flushing!
